notes: 3-pack $13.49: https://www.seeedstudio.com/Seeed-Studio-XIAO-ESP32C3-3PCS-p-5920.html — also on Amazon (B0B94JZ2YF). Onboard LiPo charging + BLE

Bare TO-92 sensors — the M0 firmware target. Power from 5V pin, open-collector output to D1 with internal pull-up (see wiring.md). Buy bare sensors, not breakout modules: module boards pull the output up to their 5V supply, which is unsafe for 3.3V GPIOs
Analog; needs a comparator for M0 pulse counting, but ideal for measuring the sensor–magnet air gap when designing the sensor arm. Honeywell-brand at Newark 09J1786 if clones disappoint
Also Amazon B09MDHBCR1 (200pcs). A3144 is unipolar — mark the working pole with a paint dot once found. Stack 2 if the air gap needs more field

M1 — skip for M0 breadboard (USB-C powers it). XIAO has bare BAT solder pads, no connector: buy wired cells and VERIFY POLARITY with a multimeter before soldering — lead colors from these vendors are not trustworthy. 401230 (4x12x30mm) also fits and is easier to find with protection PCM (e.g. B0FT3GG4ZL)
M1 — 2.54mm pitch, breadboard-friendly. Adafruit 805 is the name-brand equivalent
Models land in /models after M0 validates sensor placement
Any 400-point board + jumper kit; skip if you have them
